route.name: Stagecoach 400
route.description: Public GPX multi-day Southern California desert/mountain epic with pavement, dirt, sand, and remote segments.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX multi-day Southern California desert/mountain epic with pavement, dirt, sand, and remote segments.
route.bestSeason: Spring/fall target; July heat and seasonal access restrictions make the full route unsuitable.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX multi-day Southern California desert/mountain epic with pavement, dirt, sand, and remote segments.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Desert water logistics are a major blocker; every source must be verified.
resources.food: Sparse resupply; plan every stop and hour.
resources.camping: Full-route camp sequence exceeds the July 10-13 window and needs full legal/access planning.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Keep as a future epic, not a next-weekend option.
Do not promote the full route for July.
Use only vetted segments if needed.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: batch triage source; apply per-route overlays from this source rather than treating desert routes as July candidates
blockers: Coyote Canyon is closed to public use June 1-September 30.
Full route is 376 miles / about 5 days and too long for a normal July 10-13 trip.
Latest organizer cue sheet/private-property-sensitive reroutes are still required.
Southern California and desert routes in this batch are not ride-ready for July 10-13 unless separately rebuilt as shorter, cooler, water-secure, legally verified segments.
requiredFixes: Do not promote the full route for July.
If desired, define a short legal coastal or Cuyamaca/Descanso segment with its own GPX, camps, water, and transit checks.
For any SoCal/desert rescue attempt, produce a new segment-specific GPX, heat threshold, legal camp/lodging plan, water-gap measurement, and bailout plan.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

logistics: Final ride-ready status requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.

Subagent checked Bikepacking.com public GPX availability and July heat/fire/water constraints for Stagecoach 400, San Diego High Country, Ojai Rim, Mojave Solitaire, Death Valley Dustup, Echo-Titus, Golden Jackrabbit, Hidden in Plain Sight, and SoCal Desert Ramble.

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent reported that Anza-Borrego Coyote Canyon is closed June 1 through September 30, Death Valley July heat is extreme, and Mojave summer days are often too hot for outdoor activity. Treat desert entries as reject/rework for July unless a safer high-elevation segment is separately vetted.

promotionActions.action: Resolve target weekend fit before ride-ready promotion.
Resolve legal access before ride-ready promotion.
Resolve camping / permits before ride-ready promotion.
Resolve water / resupply before ride-ready promotion.
